title: debt_report.md summary reflects real per-gate step conclusions (fix spurious ERROR rows)
intent: Fix app.ci.generate_debt_report so its Summary table reflects the actual debt-gates step conclusions instead of rendering a spurious '| <check> | ERROR | - | - |' row for every check. Root cause: run_check ran each check subprocess with cwd=_repo_root()/services/api, but _repo_root() already resolves to services/api (it matches the nested services/api/pyproject.toml first), so the cwd became services/api/services/api (non-existent) and every subprocess raised -> ERROR. This misled a reviewer into reading a green debt-gates job as red. Fix cwd via a direct _api_dir() helper, and run the ratchet checks (complexity, file size) with their baselines so status = PASS/FAIL mirrors the gate step. No workflow, product, or gate-threshold change.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) generate_debt_report.py: added _api_dir() = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] (the services/api dir that contains the importable app package) and pointed run_check's subprocess cwd at it, replacing the broken _repo_root()/services/api (== services/api/services/api) that made every check subprocess raise and render ERROR. (2) The complexity and file-size summary rows now run in ratchet mode (pass --baseline app/ci/complexity_baseline.json / file_sizes_baseline.json), matching the debt-gates workflow step; the --json ratchet output is the NEW-violation delta, so status = PASS when 0 new (mirrors the step's success) and FAIL (N new) otherwise. Detail headers renamed 'Worst Complexity'/'Large Files' -> 'New Complexity Violations'/'New Large Files' to match the ratchet data. Added a note under the summary explaining that status mirrors the debt-gates conclusions and ERROR means the check could not be run, not that the gate failed. bare_except/fence/duplication rows unchanged (already reflect their step semantics; duplication is advisory/continue-on-error and reports WARN honestly). (3) NEW tests/test_generate_debt_report.py: locks _api_dir() shape, that run_check returns real data (not error) for a fast check, and that the summary has no spurious '| ERROR |' rows with ratchet checks showing PASS on a clean tree.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: The summary rendered ERROR for all five checks on both main and PRs even when the debt-gates job passed (every gate STEP concluded success). That directly misled a reviewer into nearly blocking a valid closure (PR #187). No existing test covered the report generator, so the cwd bug shipped silently. This makes the debt-gates report faithful to the gate it reports on and adds a regression lock.

commands_run: python -m app.ci.generate_debt_report -> Summary now shows Complexity/File size/Bare except/Safety fences = PASS, Duplication = WARN (advisory); no '| ERROR |' rows
python -m app.ci.check_complexity --baseline app/ci/complexity_baseline.json -> OK (no new violations)
python -m app.ci.check_file_sizes --baseline app/ci/file_sizes_baseline.json -> OK (no new violations)

files.intent: Fix run_check cwd (_api_dir) + run ratchet checks with baselines so the summary status mirrors the debt-gates step conclusions instead of spurious ERROR.
files.behavior_change: report-output-only - debt_report.md summary now shows real per-check status; no gate threshold or workflow change

files.intent: Regression lock: _api_dir shape, run_check returns data (cwd fix), summary has no spurious ERROR rows.
Per-PR CBSP21 manifest for this fix.
